channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump signs order to speed review of psychedelics, including the controversial drug ibogaine
item.description: President Donald Trump has directed his administration to speed up reviews of certain psychedelic drugs, including ibogaine.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Perrone And Seung Min Kim, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A big midterms year in Arizona kicks off with the state's largest county embroiled in election drama
item.description: Much of the political spotlight this election year in Arizona has been focused on the office that runs elections in Maricopa County, the state’s largest.
item.dc:creator: Josh Kelety, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump set to read Scripture from the Oval Office during 'America Reads the Bible' event starting Sunday
item.description: The president is set to read 2 Chronicles 7 during the 'America Reads the Bible' event, a passage organizers say was deliberately reserved for him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICE went on a hiring spree. Sterling credentials were not required, AP investigation finds
item.description: Some new U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers started working before passing background checks and had problems in their past. ICE announced in January that it completed an unprecedented hiring spree, adding 12,000 officers and agents to double its force.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: NPR receives $113 million in charitable gifts
item.description: "My hope is that this commitment provides the stability and the spark NPR needs to innovate boldly and strengthen its national network," says Connie Ballmer, who gave $80 million of the $113 million.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Owatonna police, fire bond question to go before voters
item.description: Officials in Owatonna say their city desperately needs new fire and police stations. But some residents question the need and the cost. Nearly 2,500 of them signed a petition to force the city to put $65 million bonding question to fund the facilities on the ballot.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers work to define grooming in new law
item.description: State lawmakers are looking at measures to prevent grooming of children by adults in schools and other institutions. Experts say understanding what grooming is and isn’t is the first step to ending this damaging facet of child abuse.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Jury finds that Live Nation acted as a monopoly and overcharged ticket buyers
item.description: An antitrust suit alleged that the company unfairly controlled too much of the live music industry at the expense of venues, artists and fans. The decision could reshape the industry.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Gomez Sarmiento, MPR News

item.title: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey named to Time 100 list
item.description: Frey’s entry in the magazine calls him the city’s “adopted son” who was drawn to Minneapolis after running a marathon. “His three terms as mayor have been an endurance test of another sort,” it reads.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota Bishop Barron says Trump owes pope an apology
item.description: Conservative Catholic Bishop Robert Barron, who is a close ally of President Trump, sharply rebuked the president for his harsh rhetoric criticizing Pope Leo XIV, calling it “inappropriate and disrespectful.
item.dc:creator: Molly Castle Work, MPR News

item.title: Special elections information for April 14
item.description: There are a number of elections happening across the state on Tuesday as voters fill a vacancy on the Columbia Heights school board and voters decide on city, school district and county referendums.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: U.S. military to block ships from Iran’s ports after peace talks fail
item.description: The U.S. Central Command said the blockade would be "enforced impartially against vessels of all nations" from 10 a.m. EDT, following the breakdown of talks after 21 hours of negotiations.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News
